data.title: Icon Offshore Changes Name
data.description:
Icon Offshore changed its name to Lianson Fleet Group, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

The offshore vessel provider's securities will be traded and quoted under the new effective Friday, it said.

data.title: Softer dollar underpins base metals, Shanghai copper hits near 3-week high
data.description:
Prices of base metals rose on Thursday, with Shanghai copper notching a near three-week high, as the dollar retreated and concerns eased about a major trade war between the United States and top metals consumer China.

The most-active copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange gained 1.4% to 76,340 yuan ($10,478.49) a metric ton by 0513 GMT, hitting its highest since January 17.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) rose 1.1% to $9,337.5 a ton.

The dollar index hovered near a one-week low. A weaker dollar makes greenback-priced commodities cheaper for holders of other currencies.

Earlier this week, China imposed tit-for-tat tariffs on some U.S. imports, escalating the trade tension between the world's two top economies, while President Donald Trump gave temporary tariff exemptions to Mexico and Canada.

Attention will also be on China to see if the country unveils more stimulus in its National People's Congress meeting in March, given the tariff war and concerns about slow demand.

"Investors appeared to take the rising trade tensions in their stride, with market activity relatively subdued. Most will be waiting for any sign of a rebound in demand following the holidays," said Daniel Hynes, senior commodity strategist and ANZ bank.

LME aluminium firmed 0.5% to $2,628.5 a ton, zinc was up 1.4% at $2,819.5, tin added 0.2% to $30,850, lead climbed 0.5% to $2,012, and nickel rose 0.9% to $15,675.

SHFE aluminium gained 0.1% to 20,290 yuan a ton, nickel climbed 2.3% to 126,010 yuan, zinc firmed 0.7% to 23,550 yuan, lead gained 0.8% to 17,135 yuan and tin advanced 2.2% to 258,810 yuan.

Meanwhile, Washington-based global security nonprofit C4ADS said Chinese firms control about 75% of Indonesia's nickel refining capacity, raising concerns over supply-chain control and environmental risks.

($1 = 7.2854 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Motwani Jadeja Foundation Enables 10 Indian Leaders to Experience Davos for the First Time
data.description:
DAVOS, Switzerland, Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The Motwani Jadeja Foundation (MJF) proudly hosted a delegation of 10 Indian leaders at Davos, Switzerland during the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting 2025.

For the first time, these leaders had the unique opportunity to participate in one of the world’s most prestigious platforms for networking and collaboration. Engaging with global stakeholders, they explored critical issues such as artificial intelligence, climate change, and sustainability.

The delegation was part of the Motwani Jadeja Global Leaders Program, an initiative designed to connect India’s top innovators and changemakers with international networks and resources to amplify their impact on a global stage.

The Motwani Jadeja Global Leaders at Davos:

Anirudh Sharma, Co-founder, Digantara

Pawan Kumar, Co-founder, Skyroot

Rahul Bhagchandani, Rajeev Circle Fellow, Motwani Jadeja Foundation, and Advisor, Gujarat Technological University

Raj Shekhar Singh, Managing Partner, Z21 Ventures

Sachindanand Swami, Founder, Invoxel

Saurav Sanyal, Managing Director, Strategica

Siddhartha Ahluwalia, Managing Partner, Neon VC

Suhani Jalota, Founder, Myna Mahila Foundation, and Hoover Fellow, Stanford University

UT Rao, Associate Professor, MICA Ahmedabad

Bollywood actor and entrepreneur Vivek Oberoi joined the delegation as a mentor, providing guidance and support to these emerging leaders during their time in Davos.

The delegation engaged with a wide range of influential figures, including government officials such as heads of state and ministers, prominent business leaders from India and around the globe, as well as global venture funds, technology innovators, and investors.

Davos, home to the annual meeting of the World Economic Forum, is widely regarded as the pinnacle of global networking opportunities, bringing together leaders from across industries, governments, and civil society. For the Motwani Jadeja Global Leaders, it provided an invaluable platform to learn, forge relationships, and explore opportunities for meaningful collaboration on a global scale.

“The Global Leaders Program is a step forward in MJF’s mission to connect India’s changemakers with global platforms,” said Asha Jadeja, founder of the Motwani Jadeja Foundation. “This first-time participation at Davos has given these leaders an incredible opportunity to engage with influential individuals and ideas shaping our future. This is India’s moment to lead, and we are proud to empower this new generation of bold, connected, and forward-thinking Indians.”

On the occasion, Vivek Oberoi remarked, “The Motwani Jadeja Foundation stands at the forefront of the global entrepreneurial revolution, empowering aspiring Indian youth through technology and exceptional guidance. I am deeply honored to have had the opportunity to mentor these young entrepreneurs at Davos. This journey has been profoundly enriching for both them and me, and I am grateful to Asha Jadeja and her exceptional team for this remarkable experience.”

The Motwani Jadeja Foundation (MJF) is committed to empowering entrepreneurs and changemakers through fellowships, investments, and partnerships. Founded by Asha Jadeja, the Foundation honors the legacy of her late husband, Stanford University’s Prof Rajeev Motwani, whose mentorship of the Google co-founders and groundbreaking contributions to technology helped shape the modern tech landscape.

MJF’s transformative initiatives, such as the Motwani Jadeja Impact Fellowship and the Motwani Jadeja India Pavilion at CES, continue to bridge India’s exceptional talent with global platforms, fostering innovation and progress for a brighter future.

Media Contact:

Programs Team, Motwani Jadeja Global Foundation

Website: http://mjf.world

Email: programs@mitli.com

Ph. No.: +919662032867

Disclaimer: This content is provided by the Motwani JadejaFoundation. The statements, views, and opinions expressed in this column are solely those of the content provider. The information shared in this press release is not a solicitation for investment, nor is it intended as investment, financial, or trading advice. It is strongly recommended that you conduct thorough research and consult with a professional financial advisor before making any investment or trading decisions. Please conduct your own research and invest at your own risk.

data.title: Saudi Industrial Investment's FY24 Net Profit Surges
data.description:
Saudi Industrial Investment Group (SASE:2250) said Thursday its net profit for the full year 2024 rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 201 million Saudi riyals, compared with 112 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 0.27 riyal from 0.15 riyal.

data.title: Market Chatter: Kerry Properties to Launch HAVA Property Project in February
data.description:
Kerry Properties is looking to launch a property project called HAVA in February, and is expected to release the price list soon, a The Standard reported Wednesday.

The property firm will open show apartments for potential buyers at the development in Yuen Long, the report said.

The firm also indicated the launch of five other projects, with around 4,200 units up for grabs, in 2025, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Waga Energy Launches New Brazilian Unit
data.description:
Waga Energy said Tuesday it established a new subsidiary in Brazil as part of its strategy to deploy its Wagabox landfill to renewable natural gas conversion technology internationally.

The renewable natural gas company's subsidiary will be headquartered in São Paulo and support the country's goals of slashing 43% of its greenhouse gas emissions by 2030, according to a Wednesday release.

PHILADELPHIA PHARMACEUTICALS INDUSTRY (PHIL.AM):

data.title: Poland's mBank returns to Q4 profit supported by one-off gain
data.description:
Commerzbank's Polish unit mBank on Thursday reported a profit for the fourth quarter, after a loss last year, helped by one-off effect of a 390 million zloty ($96.4 million) deferred tax asset and lower costs of legal risk related to foreign currency loans.

Its net profit was 986 million zlotys in the quarter, compared to a loss of 20.3 million a year earlier. That exceeded analysts' average forecast for a profit of 920 million zlotys.

($1 = 4.0463 zlotys)

data.title: Trigyn Technologies Swings to Consolidated Profit in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Trigyn Technologies posted a consolidated net profit of 12.3 million Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter, swinging from a loss of 220.2 million rupees a year ago, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

Earnings per share during the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, stood at 0.40 rupees, compared with a loss per share of 7.15 rupees a year earlier.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 dropped year on year to 2.07 billion rupees from 3.07 billion rupees.

data.title: Global Power Synergy Faces Earnings Hit From Potential Electricity Tariff Cut — Market Talk
data.description: Global Power Synergy's earnings may be impacted by a potential electricity tariff cut in Thailand, CGS International's Amornrat Cheevavichawalkul says in a research report, as the brokerage lowers its target price to THB32.50 from THB43.50. The Energy Regulatory Commission of Thailand has proposed a reduction in electricity tariff to THB3.98/unit, versus the current THB4.15/unit, by removing the adder tariff and feed-in-tariff from the electricity cost structure, the analyst notes. The brokerage estimates that every THB1/unit reduction in the electricity tariff should lead to a 12.8% decline in the Thai power company's 2025 EPS. The brokerage maintains its hold rating as concerns over potentially lower tariffs should mostly be priced in. Shares are 5.9% lower at THB28.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0549 GMT - Global Power Synergy's earnings may be impacted by a potential electricity tariff cut in Thailand, CGS International's Amornrat Cheevavichawalkul says in a research report, as the brokerage lowers its target price to THB32.50 from THB43.50. The Energy Regulatory Commission of Thailand has proposed a reduction in electricity tariff to THB3.98/unit, versus the current THB4.15/unit, by removing the adder tariff and feed-in-tariff from the electricity cost structure, the analyst notes. The brokerage estimates that every THB1/unit reduction in the electricity tariff should lead to a 12.8% decline in the Thai power company's 2025 EPS. The brokerage maintains its hold rating as concerns over potentially lower tariffs should mostly be priced in. Shares are 5.9% lower at THB28.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0545 GMT - Samsung Heavy Industries is expected to exceed its 2025 guidance for new contract wins, Nomura analyst Eon Hwang writes in a note. Hwang expects new orders for the South Korean shipbuilder to jump 59% to $12 billion this year, beating its own target of $9.8 billion. Samsung Heavy's operating profit is projected to rise 54% in 2025, Hwang says. He is also upbeat on Samsung Heavy bidding for two floating liquefied natural gas projects this year--one off Mozambique and the other in the U.S. Nomura raises its target price for the stock by 14% to KRW16,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.8% lower at KRW13,160. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0512 GMT - The scenario for Brent crude oil at $60-$65 per barrel through 2H just got stronger, Citi Research's Francesco Martoccia says in a research report. An increase in non-OPEC+ oil supply remains poised to outpace weak global demand growth from 2Q through 4Q, with the oil market shifting into surplus, the energy strategist says. "We remain strongly of the view that President Trump could ultimately prove to be a bearish influence on the oil market," Martoccia says. He notes that Trump has consistently highlighted lower energy prices as the central solution to issues such as U.S. inflation. Front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.1% higher at $74.69/bbl; front-month WTI crude oil futures are 0.2% higher at $71.18/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Major Shareholder Announcement
data.description:
Penneo

Company Release no. 21/2025

Copenhagen, Denmark, 6 February 2025

Pursuant to Section 30 of the Consolidated Act no. 198 of 26 February 2024 on capital markets, as amended, Penneo A/S hereby announces that on 5 February 2025, Penneo A/S was informed that as of 3 February 2025, Visma Danmark Holding A/S has acquired 32,705,282 shares in Penneo A/S, corresponding to 91.51% of the entire share capital and voting rights in Penneo.

For further information, please see the attached notification form. 

Further information

CEO, Christian Stendevad

Mobile (+45) 27 29 50 02

About Penneo

Penneo combines digital signing and Know Your Customer (KYC) workflows into a single platform that streamlines critical business processes for anti-money-laundering regulated B2B companies.

Founded in Copenhagen and operating throughout Europe, Penneo is fast becoming the go-to platform for companies looking to save time on administrative tasks while ensuring complete regulatory compliance and peace of mind for themselves and their clients. Penneo is already trusted by +3,000 companies - including the world's leading auditors.

For information about Penneo, please visit https://penneo.com/investors/.

https://news.cision.com/penneo/r/major-shareholder-announcement%2Cc4101494

data.title: Resouro Strategic Metals Reports High-Grade Drilling Results from Brazil Titanium, Rare Earth Elements Project
data.description:
Resouro Strategic Metals reported high-grade results from a drilling program at its Tiros titanium and rare earth elements project in Brazil, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

It reported intercepts of six meters at 19.5% grade of titanium oxide and 4,800 parts-per-million grade of total rare earth oxides from 39 meters down-hole and seven meters at 24.5% grade of titanium oxide and 11,000 ppm grade of total rare earth oxides from 39 meters down-hole.

The program consisted of infill diamond drilling, with 39 holes drilled to a total depth of 2,519 meters. Further assays are expected in February, the filing noted.

The firm's shares rose over 7% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: Malawi Holds Key Policy Rate at 26%
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of Malawi announced that it kept its benchmark lending rate steady at 26% in its decision on January 30, 2025.

The Committee also maintained the lombard rate at 20 basis points above the policy rate and retained the liquidity reserve requirement ratio at 10% for local currency deposits and 3.75% for foreign currency deposits.

The Committee noted that inflation eased in Q4 2024 and is expected to decline further in 2025, allowing room for possible rate cuts.

However, food inflation rose in December due to maize shortages.

Inflation is projected to slow to 22% by year-end, supported by base effects and monetary policy.

The MPC stressed the need for measures to boost productivity and forex reserves.

Meanwhile, GDP growth is forecast to rebound to 4.0% in 2025 from 1.8% in 2024, driven by agriculture, mining, and tourism.

data.title: Market Chatter: Tesla Rolls Out New Subsidies As Part of Lunar New Year Promotions
data.description:
US electric vehicle company Tesla is introducing more new subsidies in mainland China in a bid to win against domestic EV giants in the country during the Lunar New Year, the South China Morning Post reported Wednesday.

Buyers of the Model 3 are eligible for an 8,000-yuan vehicle insurance subsidy, while Tesla will also offer a five-year interest-free loan for eligible customers, the report said.

Tesla said the promotion is the biggest for its Shanghai-made Model 3 units, the SCMP said.

China's top new-energy vehicle manufacturers include BYD , Li Auto , XPeng and NIO .

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Australia shares sidestep trade war fears; News Corp gains on upbeat results
data.description:
Key points:
  • ASX 200 up over 1%
  • CBA hits record high
  • News Corp's Australia shares surge 5.8%

Australian stocks gained more than 1% on Thursday, led by a surge in News Corp's local shares on upbeat results, as investors shrugged off immediate fears of a global trade war and keenly awaited the upcoming earnings season.

The S&P/ASX 200 index closed at 8,520.7 points, just under 12 points shy of its all-time high of 8,532.3.

Traders looked past the trade tumult that weighed on the financial markets this week following U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs and China's retaliatory action.

Corporate earnings in Australia will begin next week, and while modest growth is expected, traders will scrutinise whether profits justify the stretched valuations, particularly in the face of challenges including U.S. tariffs.

Local economic data over the last few months has shown that the economy has not only prevented a recession, but has also been doing well across various different data point.

Richard Schellbach, a strategist with UBS, expects the results and outlook statements to reflect the improved set of conditions that formed through the December-quarter such as easing inflation, jobs strength, higher wage income and growth in retail sales.

Australia-listed shares of News Corp , rose 5.8% to top the benchmark index after the global media giant's second-quarter earnings beat analysts' estimates.

Banks led sectoral gains, ending 2% higher in their biggest daily rise in three weeks. The Commonwealth Bank of Australia , due to report its earnings on February 12, hit a record high.

"The expectation is that results will be pretty good and that's why you see a bit more positivity coming into the market and banks, being one of the largest sector, doing quite well today," said Jun Bei Liu, portfolio manager at Tribeca Investment Partners.

Consumer discretionary stocks (.AXDJ) also gained, adding 1.9%, with Wesfarmers ending 3.2% higher after UBS upgraded the conglomerate's stock to "neutral" from "sell" on expectations of strong performance at hardware chain Bunnings.

"The expectation is for the Reserve Bank of Australia to cut (rates) in the following week and these consumer names have rallied quite aggressively into this cut," Liu said.

Among other sub-indexes, miners advanced 0.8%, although energy stocks fell 0.3% on lower underlying oil prices.

New Zealand's financial markets were closed for a public holiday.

data.title: Micro-X Partners Enters Security Partnership with Malaysian Firm with AU$2.4 Million Equity Investment
data.description:
Micro-X signed two agreements to form a security partnership with Billion Prima, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Malaysian hi-tech baggage, parcel, and vehicle scanning specialist Billion Prima will make a AU$2.4 million strategic equity investment, subscribing for 26.7 million shares at AU$0.09 per share.

The two firms entered a AU$3.2 million agreement to commercialize a baggage and parcel scanning unit, developed using Micro-X NEX technology with Billion Prima to receive exclusive rights to manufacture and sell the unit in Southeast Asia for 20 years.

Billion Prima will also deliver a AU$1 million first payment of the commercialization project to the cold cathode x-ray technology company.

Micro-X will retain the right to sell the unit outside of Southeast Asia and to license the technology to third parties in other regions.

data.title: Ikonisys' Hospitex Books Contract to Supply IT, Networking Systems to Hospitals in Ethiopia
data.description:
Ikonisys said Wednesday that its Italian medical devices unit Hospitex received an order to deliver information technology and networking systems to hospitals in Adwa, Ethiopia.

The French diagnostics company said the United Nations will finance the project, which aims to boost hospital connectivity and digital healthcare services in Ethiopia.

data.title: New York Times business news - Feb 6
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- The U.S. Postal Service said it will resume accepting all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong on Wednesday, a day after temporarily suspending such service.

- Alphabet's Google has scrapped its goal to hire more employees from underrepresented groups and is reviewing some of its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives, joining a slew of U.S. businesses scaling back diversity initiatives.

- The Justice Department said it would not bring charges against anyone affiliated with the group Project Veritas over their role in trying to publish the contents of a diary that had been stolen from Ashley Biden, daughter of former U.S. President Joe Biden.

- Elon Musk said that U.S. President Donald Trump's cost-cutting group, the Department of Government Efficiency, will work to "make rapid safety upgrades to the air traffic control system."

data.title: Transtek Medical Electronics' Blood Monitoring Product Gets US FDA's Approval for Registration; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Guangdong Transtek Medical Electronics' blood glucose monitoring products received a registration certificate from the US Food and Drug Administration, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

The product is used to detect the glucose concentration of a diabetic patient, the Chinese medical supply company said.

The company's shares jumped over 5% in recent trade.

data.title: CGS International Research Adjusts Lendlease Global Commercial REIT's Price Target to SG$0.69 from SG$0.71, Keeps at Add
data.description: Lendlease Global Commercial REIT has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from SG$0.57 to SG$0.77, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: RAK Properties, Four Seasons to Develop New Luxury Resort in UAE
data.description:
RAK Properties (ADX:RAKPROP) and Canadian luxury hotel company Four Seasons plan to develop a beach resort and private residences at the coastal destination of Mina in Ras Al Khaimah, United Arab Emirates.

The Four Seasons Resort and Residences Ras Al Khaimah will comprise 150 rooms, suites and signature villas, as well as 130 private residences, 10 treatment rooms, among other amenities, according to a Tuesday release.

Residential sales are expected to begin at the end of 2025, with construction to commence in early 2026, the UAE-based property developer's Chief Executive Officer Sameh Muhtadi said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Xiaomi to Launch Luxury Vehicle in February; Aims To Sell 10,000 Units in 2025
data.description:
Xiaomi is planning to launch the SU7 Ultra, its luxury vehicle, at the end of February, with an aim to sell 10,000 units in 2025, Reuters reported citing founder Lei Jun on Wednesday.

Lei Jun provided the update on his Red Note account the same day.

Shares of the company were up 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Japan 10-Year Yield Hovers Near 14-Year High
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield eased to around 1.27% on Thursday, but remained near a 14-year high reached in the previous session.

This follows comments from Bank of Japan board member Naoki Tamura, who stated that the central bank should raise the policy rate to at least 1% in the latter half of fiscal 2025.

Finance Minister Katsunobu Kato also warned that inflation could continue to rise.

Data released earlier this week showed strong wage growth, which further fueled expectations that the BOJ will continue raising interest rates this year.

Real wages in Japan rose for the second consecutive month in December, with nominal wage growth hitting a near three-decade high, driven largely by a surge in winter bonuses.

The BOJ raised interest rates in January and indicated its readiness to hike further if economic and price trends align with its forecasts.

data.title: Advanced Systems Automation Extends Exclusivity Period for Disposal of Stake in Emerald Precision Engineering
data.description:
Advanced Systems Automation and Ethereal Capital agreed to extend the exclusivity period under the term sheet for the disposal of 67% of the company's stake in Emerald Precision Engineering from Jan. 7 to March 14.

In an earlier filing, the company had entered into a non-binding term sheet with Ethereal Capital to sell a 67% stake in its subsidiary, Emerald Precision Engineering, for 22 million ringgit.

The deal also includes the issuance of 15 million irredeemable convertible preference shares at 1 ringgit each in Emerald Precision Engineering to the purchaser, a filing with the Singapore Exchange said on Wednesday.
